sentences.sentence: Americans today choose among more options in more parts of life than has ever been possible before.
sentences.translation: 如今，美国人在生活中更多领域拥有的选择，比以往任何时候都多。

structure.text: Americans today
choose among more options in more parts of life
than has ever been possible before

difficult_points: than 引导比较结构，比较如今的选择范围与过去曾经可能达到的范围。
than 从句省略了与主句重复的具体选择范围。

sentences.sentence: To an extent, the opportunity to choose enhances our lives.
sentences.translation: 在一定程度上，拥有选择的机会改善了我们的生活。

structure.text: To an extent
the opportunity to choose
enhances our lives

difficult_points: to choose 是不定式作后置定语，说明 opportunity 的内容。
To an extent 限定整句判断，表明积极作用并非毫无限制。

sentences.sentence: It is only logical to think that if some choices are good, more is better; people who care about having infinite options will benefit from them, and those who do not can always just ignore the 273 versions of cereal they have never tried.
sentences.translation: 很自然会让人认为：如果有一些选择是好事，那么选择越多就越好；在意拥有无限选项的人会从中受益，而不在意的人大可以忽略那273种他们从未尝试过的麦片。

structure.text: It is only logical to think
that if some choices are good, more is better
people who care about having infinite options will benefit from them
and those who do not can always just ignore the 273 versions of cereal they have never tried

difficult_points: It 是形式主语，真正内容由 to think 及其后的 that 从句表达。
who care about ... 与 who do not 分别限定 people 和 those，后者省略重复内容。
they have never tried 是省略关系代词的定语从句，修饰 cereal 的品种。

sentences.sentence: Yet recent research strongly suggests that, psychologically, this assumption is wrong, with 5% lower percentage announcing they are happy.
sentences.translation: 然而，近期研究有力地表明，从心理层面看，这一假设是错误的；宣称自己幸福的人所占比例低了5%。

structure.text: recent research
strongly suggests
that, psychologically, this assumption is wrong
with 5% lower percentage announcing they are happy

difficult_points: that 引导宾语从句，psychologically 是插入性状语。
with 复合结构补充研究结果，announcing 的逻辑主语是 percentage。
5% lower percentage 是源文不自然的统计表达，按“比例低5%”理解。

sentences.sentence: Although some choices are undoubtedly better than none, more is not always better than less.
sentences.translation: 尽管有一些选择无疑比毫无选择更好，但选择更多并不总比选择更少好。

structure.text: Although some choices are undoubtedly better than none
more
is not always better than less

difficult_points: Although 引导让步从句，先承认有选择优于没选择。
more 和 less 都省略了 choices，形成简洁比较。

sentences.sentence: Recent research offers insight into why many people end up unhappy rather than pleased when their options expand.
sentences.translation: 近期研究帮助我们理解了，为何许多人在选择增多时最终感到不快而非满意。

structure.text: Recent research
offers insight into
why many people end up unhappy rather than pleased
when their options expand

difficult_points: why 从句作介词 into 的宾语，说明研究解释的问题。
end up 后接形容词表示最终状态，rather than 构成对比。

sentences.sentence: We began by making a distinction between "maximizers" (those who always aim to make the best possible choice) and "satisficers" (those who aim for "good enough," whether or not better selections might be out there).
sentences.translation: 我们首先区分了“最大化者”（总想做出最佳选择的人）和“满足者”（以“足够好”为目标的人，不论外面是否可能还有更好的选择）。

structure.text: We
began by making a distinction between "maximizers"
those who always aim to make the best possible choice
and "satisficers" (those who aim for "good enough," whether or not better selections might be out there)

difficult_points: by making ... 表示 begin 的方式，between A and B 构成成对结构。
两个 those 是术语的同位解释，分别由 who 从句限定。
whether or not 表示无论是否还有更好的选项。

sentences.sentence: In particular, we composed a set of statements—the Maximization Scale—to diagnose people's propensity to maximize.
sentences.translation: 具体来说，我们编制了一组陈述题——“最大化量表”——用于诊断人们追求最大化的倾向。

structure.text: we
composed a set of statements
the Maximization Scale
to diagnose people's propensity to maximize

difficult_points: the Maximization Scale 是插入的同位语，为前面的陈述题组命名。
to diagnose 是目的状语，propensity 后的 to maximize 说明倾向的内容。

sentences.sentence: Then we had several thousand people rate themselves from 1 to 7(from "completely disagree" to "completely agree") on such statements as "I never settle for second best."
sentences.translation: 接着，我们让数千人针对“我从不满足于次优选择”之类的陈述，从1到7给自己评分（从“完全不同意”到“完全同意”）。

structure.text: we
had several thousand people rate themselves
from 1 to 7(from "completely disagree" to "completely agree")
on such statements as "I never settle for second best."

difficult_points: have + 宾语 + 动词原形是使役结构，表示让数千人评分。
such ... as 引出陈述题的例子。
7(from 是源文缺少空格的排版异常，语义仍是评分范围的括注。

sentences.sentence: We also evaluated their sense of satisfaction with their decisions.
sentences.translation: 我们还评估了他们对自己所作决定的满意程度。

structure.text: We
also evaluated
their sense of satisfaction with their decisions

difficult_points: sense of satisfaction 表示满意感或满意程度，核心词是 sense。
with their decisions 修饰 satisfaction，说明满意所针对的对象。

sentences.sentence: We did not define a sharp cutoff to separate maximizers from satisficers, but in general, we think of individuals whose average scores are higher than 4 (the scale's midpoint) as maxi- misers and those whose scores are lower than the midpoint as satisficers.
sentences.translation: 我们没有划定明确分界线来区分最大化者和满足者；但总体而言，我们把平均得分高于4分（量表中点）的人视为最大化者，把得分低于中点的人视为满足者。

structure.text: We did not define a sharp cutoff to separate maximizers from satisficers
but in general, we think of individuals whose average scores are higher than 4 (the scale's midpoint) as maxi- misers
whose average scores are higher than 4
and those whose scores are lower than the midpoint as satisficers

difficult_points: think of A as B 表示把A视为B，后面并列两个 A-as-B 结构。
两个 whose 从句分别限定 individuals 和 those。
maxi- misers 是源文断词排版异常，语义上等同 maximizers。

sentences.sentence: People who score highest on the test—the greatest maximizers—engage in more product comparisons than the lowest scorers, both before and after they make purchasing decisions, and they take longer to decide what to buy.
sentences.translation: 测试得分最高的人——也就是最典型的最大化者——比得分最低者进行更多产品比较，无论在购买决策之前还是之后，而且他们要花更长时间决定买什么。

structure.text: People who score highest on the test
the greatest maximizers
engage in more product comparisons than the lowest scorers
and they take longer to decide what to buy

difficult_points: who score highest ... 修饰 People，破折号间是同位语。
both before and after 后接 they make purchasing decisions，表示决策前后。
what to buy 是疑问词加不定式，作 decide 的宾语。

sentences.sentence: When satisficers find an item that meets their standards, they stop looking.
sentences.translation: 当满足者找到符合自己标准的商品时，他们就不再寻找了。

structure.text: When satisficers find an item that meets their standards
that meets their standards
they stop looking

difficult_points: When 从句说明停止搜索的时点，也带有条件意味。
that meets their standards 修饰 an item，that 在从句中作主语。
stop doing 表示停止正在做的事。

sentences.sentence: But maximizers exert enormous effort reading labels, checking out consumer magazines and trying new products.
sentences.translation: 但最大化者会投入巨大精力阅读标签、查阅消费杂志并试用新产品。

structure.text: maximizers
exert enormous effort
reading labels, checking out consumer magazines and trying new products

difficult_points: reading、checking out 和 trying 是并列分词短语，说明精力花在何处。
check out 在此表示查看、查阅，而不是结账离开。

sentences.sentence: They also spend more time comparing their purchasing decisions with those of others.
sentences.translation: 他们还会花更多时间把自己的购买决定与他人的决定作比较。

structure.text: They
also spend more time
comparing their purchasing decisions with those of others

difficult_points: spend time doing 表示花时间做某事，comparing 是动名词。
those 替代 purchasing decisions，避免重复，并非指人。

sentences.sentence: We found that the greatest maximizers are the least happy with the fruits of their efforts.
sentences.translation: 我们发现，最典型的最大化者对自己努力取得的成果最不满意。

structure.text: We found
that the greatest maximizers are the least happy with the fruits of their efforts

difficult_points: that 从句作 found 的宾语，the least happy 表示最不满意。
the fruits of their efforts 是比喻，指努力带来的成果。

sentences.sentence: When they compare themselves with others, they get little pleasure from finding out that they did better and substantial dissatisfaction from finding out that they did worse.
sentences.translation: 当他们把自己与他人比较时，发现自己表现更好并不会带来多少快乐，而发现自己更差却会带来强烈不满。

structure.text: When they compare themselves with others
they get little pleasure from finding out that they did better
and substantial dissatisfaction from finding out that they did worse

difficult_points: little pleasure 与 substantial dissatisfaction 构成对比。
两个 finding out 后的 that 从句说明发现内容，第二个并列部分省略 get。

sentences.sentence: They are more prone to experiencing regret after a purchase, and if their acquisition disappoints them, their sense of well-being takes longer to recover.
sentences.translation: 他们购物后更容易后悔；如果买到的东西令他们失望，其幸福感也需要更久才能恢复。

structure.text: They are more prone to experiencing regret after a purchase
if their acquisition disappoints them
their sense of well-being takes longer to recover

difficult_points: be prone to 中 to 是介词，后接动名词。
acquisition 在此指买到的东西；if 从句说明恢复更慢的条件。

sentences.sentence: They also tend to brood or ruminate more than satisficers do.
sentences.translation: 他们也往往比满足者更容易反复忧思。

structure.text: They also tend to brood or ruminate
more than satisficers do

difficult_points: brood 和 ruminate 均指反复思虑不快之事。
than satisficers do 中 do 代替前面的 brood or ruminate。

sentences.sentence: Does it follow that maximizers are less happy in general than satisficers?
sentences.translation: 那么，是否可以由此得出结论：总体而言，最大化者不如满足者幸福？

structure.text: Does it follow
that maximizers are less happy in general than satisficers

difficult_points: Does it follow that 意为是否可由此得出某结论。
it 是形式主语，that 从句承载真正讨论的结论。

sentences.sentence: We tested this by having people fill out a variety of questionnaires known to be reliable indicators of wellbeing.
sentences.translation: 我们让人们填写多种被公认为能可靠反映幸福感的问卷，以此检验这一点。

structure.text: We tested this
by having people fill out a variety of questionnaires
known to be reliable indicators of wellbeing

difficult_points: by having ... 表示检验方法，having people fill out 是使役结构。
known to be ... 是过去分词短语，后置修饰 questionnaires。

sentences.sentence: As might be expected, individuals with high maximization scores experienced less satisfaction with life and were less happy, less optimistic and more depressed than people with low maximization scores.
sentences.translation: 正如预期，最大化得分高的人生活满意度较低，而且与得分低的人相比，他们更不幸福、更不乐观，也更抑郁。

structure.text: As might be expected
individuals with high maximization scores
experienced less satisfaction with life
and were less happy, less optimistic and more depressed than people with low maximization scores

difficult_points: As might be expected 是评注性从句，表示结果符合预期。
experienced 与 were 是并列谓语，三个形容词比较项共同与低分人群对比。

sentences.sentence: Indeed, those with extreme maximization ratings had depression scores that placed them in the borderline of clinical range.
sentences.translation: 事实上，那些最大化倾向评分极高的人，其抑郁评分已使他们处在临床范围的临界线上。

structure.text: those with extreme maximization ratings
had depression scores
that placed them in the borderline of clinical range

difficult_points: those 指具有极高最大化评分的人。
that 从句修饰 depression scores，并在从句中作主语。

sentences.sentence: Several factors explain why more choice is not always better than less, especially for maxi misers.
sentences.translation: 有几个因素可以解释为何选择更多并不总比选择更少好，尤其对最大化者而言。

structure.text: Several factors
explain
why more choice is not always better than less

difficult_points: why 从句作 explain 的宾语，less 省略 choice。
maxi misers 是源文把 maximizers 错误拆开的排版异常。

sentences.sentence: High among these are "opportunity costs."
sentences.translation: 其中尤为重要的是“机会成本”。

structure.text: High among these
are
"opportunity costs."

difficult_points: 表语 High among these 前置，引起主谓倒装。
High 表示位居重要位置，而非物理高度。

sentences.sentence: The quality of any given option cannot be assessed in isolation from its alternatives.
sentences.translation: 任何一个既定选项的质量，都不能脱离其他备选方案单独评估。

structure.text: The quality of any given option
cannot be assessed
in isolation from its alternatives

difficult_points: cannot be assessed 是情态动词加被动语态。
in isolation from 表示脱离其他事物孤立看待，its 指该选项。

sentences.sentence: One of the "costs" of making a selection is losing the opportunities that a different option would have afforded.
sentences.translation: 作出选择的一项“代价”，就是失去另一选项本来可能带来的机会。

structure.text: One of the "costs" of making a selection
is losing the opportunities
that a different option would have afforded

difficult_points: losing 是动名词作表语，用来定义其中一项成本。
that 修饰 opportunities；would have afforded 表示本来会提供。

sentences.sentence: Thus, an opportunity cost of vacationing on the beach in Cape Cod might be missing the fabulous restaurants in the Napa Valley.
sentences.translation: 因此，选择去科德角海滩度假的一项机会成本，可能就是错过纳帕谷的绝佳餐厅。

structure.text: an opportunity cost of vacationing on the beach in Cape Cod
might be
missing the fabulous restaurants in the Napa Valley

difficult_points: of vacationing ... 说明是哪项选择产生的机会成本。
missing 是动名词作表语，指因选一个方案而错过另一个。

sentences.sentence: Early Decision Making Research by Daniel Kahneman and Amos Tversky showed that people respond much more strongly to losses than gains.
sentences.translation: 卡尼曼和特沃斯基早期关于决策的研究表明，人们对损失的反应远强于对收益的反应。

structure.text: Early Decision Making Research by Daniel Kahneman and Amos Tversky
showed
that people respond much more strongly to losses than gains

difficult_points: by ... 修饰 Research，说明研究者。
than gains 后省略与前面重复的 respond to。

sentences.sentence: If we assume that opportunity costs reduce the overall desirability of the most preferred choice, then the more alternatives there are, the deeper our sense of loss will be and the less satisfaction we will derive from our ultimate decision.
sentences.translation: 如果我们假定机会成本会降低首选方案的整体吸引力，那么备选方案越多，我们的损失感就越强，从最终决定中获得的满意度也越低。

structure.text: If we assume that opportunity costs reduce the overall desirability of the most preferred choice
the more alternatives there are
the less satisfaction we will derive from our ultimate decision

difficult_points: If 从句包含 that 宾语从句，说明假设内容。
the more ... the deeper ... and the less ... 是越……越……结构，后两项并列。

sentences.sentence: The problem of opportunity costs will be better for a satisficer.
sentences.translation: 对满足者来说，机会成本问题会比较容易应对。

structure.text: The problem of opportunity costs
will be better

difficult_points: better 表示该问题对满足者而言较轻。
for a satisficer 指明比较有利的对象。

sentences.sentence: The latter's "good enough"philosophy can survive thoughts about opportunity costs.
sentences.translation: 后者奉行的“足够好”理念，经得住对机会成本的考量。

structure.text: The latter's "good enough"philosophy
can survive thoughts about opportunity costs

difficult_points: The latter 指前一句的 satisficer。
survive 是比喻；引号后缺空格是源文排版异常。

sentences.sentence: In addition, the "good enough"standard leads to much less searching and inspection of alternatives than the maximizer's"best" standard.
sentences.translation: 此外，与最大化者的“最佳”标准相比，“足够好”标准会让人少做很多搜索和备选方案审查。

structure.text: the "good enough"standard
leads to much less searching and inspection of alternatives
than the maximizer's"best" standard

difficult_points: lead to 后接并列动名词 searching 和 inspection。
than 比较两种标准；两处引号后缺空格是源文异常。

sentences.sentence: With fewer choices under consideration, a person will have fewer opportunity costs to subtract.
sentences.translation: 需要考虑的选择更少，一个人要扣除的机会成本也就更少。

structure.text: With fewer choices under consideration
a person
will have fewer opportunity costs to subtract

difficult_points: under consideration 是过去分词短语，修饰 choices。
to subtract 是不定式后置修饰 opportunity costs。

sentences.sentence: Just as people feel sorrow about the opportunities they have forgone, they may also suffer regret about the option they settled on.
sentences.translation: 正如人们会为已经放弃的机会感到遗憾，他们也可能为最终选定的方案后悔。

structure.text: Just as people feel sorrow about the opportunities they have forgone
they may also suffer regret about the option they settled on

difficult_points: Just as 引导类比从句，连接两种相似的遗憾。
they have forgone 和 they settled on 都是省略关系代词的定语从句。

sentences.sentence: My colleagues and I devised a scale to measure proneness to feeling regret, and we found that people with high sensitivity to regret are less happy, less satisfied with life, less optimistic and more depressed than those with low sensitivity.
sentences.translation: 我和同事设计了一个量表来衡量后悔倾向，并发现对后悔高度敏感的人比敏感度低的人更不幸福、更不满意生活、更不乐观，也更抑郁。

structure.text: My colleagues and I devised a scale to measure proneness to feeling regret
and we found
that people with high sensitivity to regret are less happy, less satisfied with life, less optimistic and more depressed than those with low sensitivity

difficult_points: to measure 是目的状语，proneness to 中 to 是介词。
that 从句包含四个并列表语；those 替代 people。

sentences.sentence: Not surprisingly, we also found that people with high regret sensitivity tend to be maximizers.
sentences.translation: 不出所料，我们还发现，对后悔高度敏感的人往往是最大化者。

structure.text: we also found
that people with high regret sensitivity tend to be maximizers

difficult_points: Not surprisingly 是评注性状语，评价发现符合预期。
that 从句作 found 的宾语，tend to 表示一般倾向。

sentences.sentence: Indeed, we think that worry over future regret is a major reason that individuals become maximizers.
sentences.translation: 事实上，我们认为，对未来后悔的担忧是人们成为最大化者的一个主要原因。

structure.text: we think
that worry over future regret is a major reason
that individuals become maximizers

difficult_points: 第一个 that 引导 think 的宾语从句。
第二个 that 从句修饰 reason，说明具体原因所导致的结果。

sentences.sentence: The only way to be sure you will not regret a decision is by making the best possible one.
sentences.translation: 要确保自己不会后悔，唯一的方法就是作出尽可能好的决定。

structure.text: The only way to be sure you will not regret a decision
is
by making the best possible one

difficult_points: to be sure 修饰 way，其后省略 that 的从句说明确信内容。
one 替代 decision；by making 表示实现该目标的方式。

sentences.sentence: Unfortunately, the more options you have and the more opportunity costs you incur, the more likely you are to experience regret.
sentences.translation: 遗憾的是，你拥有的选项越多、承担的机会成本越大，就越可能感到后悔。

structure.text: the more options you have
and the more opportunity costs you incur
the more likely you are to experience regret

difficult_points: the more ... the more ... 是“越……越……”关联比较结构。
前半部分并列两个条件维度：选项数量和机会成本。

sentences.sentence: In a classic demonstration of the power of sunk costs, people were offered season subscrip tions to a local theatre company.
sentences.translation: 在一个展示沉没成本力量的经典实验中，人们获得了当地一家剧团的整季订票方案。

structure.text: In a classic demonstration of the power of sunk costs
people
were offered season subscrip tions to a local theatre company

difficult_points: were offered 是双宾语结构的被动形式，people 是接受者。
subscrip tions 是源文把 subscriptions 错误断开的排版异常。

sentences.sentence: Some were offered the tickets at full price and others at a discount.
sentences.translation: 一些人拿到的是全价票，另一些人则以折扣价购票。

structure.text: Some were offered the tickets at full price
and others at a discount

difficult_points: Some ... and others ... 构成两组实验对象的对照。
第二个并列分句省略 were offered the tickets。

sentences.sentence: Then the researchers simply kept track of how often the ticket purchasers actually attended the plays over the course of the season.
sentences.translation: 随后，研究人员只是持续记录购票者在整个演出季实际观看演出的频率。

structure.text: the researchers
simply kept track of
how often the ticket purchasers actually attended the plays
over the course of the season

difficult_points: how often 从句作介词 of 的宾语，表示被记录的是出席频率。
over the course of 表示贯穿整个时间段。

sentences.sentence: Full-price payers were more likely to show up at performances than discount payers.
sentences.translation: 全价购票者比折扣购票者更可能到场观看演出。

structure.text: Full-price payers
were more likely to show up at performances
than discount payers

difficult_points: be more likely to 表示更可能做某事。
than 后省略 were likely to show up at performances。

sentences.sentence: The reason for this, the investigators argued, was that the full-price payers would experience more regret if they did not use the tickets because not using the more costly tickets would constitute a bigger loss.
sentences.translation: 研究人员认为，原因在于：全价购票者若不使用门票会更加后悔，因为不用价格更高的票意味着更大的损失。

structure.text: The reason for this
the investigators argued
was that the full-price payers would experience more regret
if they did not use the tickets because not using the more costly tickets would constitute a bigger loss

difficult_points: the investigators argued 是插入语。
that 引导表语从句；if 从句中的 because 再说明后悔原因。
not using ... 是动名词短语作主语。

sentences.sentence: To increase sense of happiness, we can decide to restrict our options when the decision is not crucial.
sentences.translation: 为了提升幸福感，我们可以在决定并不关键时限制自己的选项。

structure.text: To increase sense of happiness
we can decide to restrict our options
when the decision is not crucial

difficult_points: To increase ... 是不定式目的状语。
when 从句限定适合限制选择的情形。

sentences.sentence: For example, make a rule to visit no more than two stores when shopping for clothing.
sentences.translation: 例如，可以规定自己买衣服时最多只逛两家商店。

structure.text: make a rule
to visit no more than two stores
when shopping for clothing

difficult_points: 句子是祈使句，省略主语 you。
when shopping ... 是省略 you are 的时间从句。
